security_findings.issue: Ollama auth token may be exposed in process list if passed via environment
security_findings.description: MANUSAT_OLLAMA_TOKEN is passed to limbs/ollama.sh without process filtering. Could leak in `ps aux` output.
security_findings.recommendation: Use credentials file (mode 0600) or encrypted vault instead of env vars for long-lived tokens

security_findings.issue: TTL computation uses `date -d` which may not be portable
security_findings.description: Line 54 uses GNU `date -d` which fails on BSD/macOS. No fallback to gdate or pure bash arithmetic.
security_findings.recommendation: Use pure bash arithmetic ($(( $(date +%s) + ttl ))) for portability

security_findings.issue: No cleanup handler for background processes spawned by agents
security_findings.description: Background agent tasks lack explicit cleanup on SIGTERM/SIGINT. May leave orphaned processes.
security_findings.recommendation: Add trap handlers and cleanup_background_tasks() before any background spawning

security_findings.issue: Backup filenames use epoch timestamp but collisions possible in rapid execution
security_findings.description: Line 29: `${FILE}.bak.$(date +%s)` uses 1-second granularity; simultaneous edits may collide.
security_findings.recommendation: Use `date +%s%N` (nanosecond precision) or UUID for unique backups

quality_findings.issue: JIT-020 sed injection fix is correctly implemented
quality_findings.description: Replaced vulnerable sed-based string replacement with Python's str.replace() literal semantics. Properly escapes all shell inputs.
quality_findings.recommendation: Good pattern - continue using Python for string ops in bash scripts

quality_findings.issue: HMAC-SHA256 message signing is correctly implemented
quality_findings.description: JIT-011 uses canonical string format (no separators), openssl dgst, and proper audit logging. Supports both strict and legacy modes.
quality_findings.recommendation: Implementation matches JWT best practices. Monitor AUTH_LOG for failures.

quality_findings.issue: Comprehensive validation library with regex patterns for all common types
quality_findings.description: Email, URL, alphanum, filename, JSON, command, agent_name, path, integer validators all use appropriate regex and length checks.
quality_findings.recommendation: Continue using validate() wrapper for all external inputs

quality_findings.issue: Security test suites are comprehensive (24 cases across 6 categories)
quality_findings.description: Crypto, auth, injection, XSS, path traversal, deserialization test vectors are present. Fast execution mode enabled.
quality_findings.recommendation: Extend to CI/CD pipeline and run on every commit

quality_findings.issue: Protocol documentation is clear and comprehensive
quality_findings.description: Message bus protocol v1.0 is documented with examples, TTL defaults, DLQ behavior. API docs include integration guide.
quality_findings.recommendation: Continue updating as new features are added

immediate: Move Ollama token to credential file (mode 0600) instead of env var
short_term: Add trap handlers for signal cleanup in background agent spawning
Integrate security test suites into CI/CD pipeline
long_term: Implement secrets rotation policy for MANUSAT_BUS_SECRET
Add rate-limiting to bus.sh to prevent message flooding
Extend DLQ replay with exponential backoff strategy

next_steps: 1. Create ticket for credential management refactor (HIGH)
2. Add portability tests for BSD/macOS compatibility (MEDIUM)
4. Add CI/CD integration for security test suites (LOW)
